content.title: VBScript Is Becoming a Platform Risk
content.subtitle: 2026: The Migration Planning Year for SAP Users
content.message: Microsoft has deprecated VBScript. For SAP automation teams, this turns legacy GUI scripts from a convenience into a critical business continuity risk.

content.title: Why SAP Users Are Exposed
content.subtitle: Invisible dependencies outside the strategic stack
keyPoints: Legacy reliance on .vbs launchers for unattended SAP GUI scripting.
Complex dependencies between Excel/VBA, COM objects, and file handling.
Undocumented desktop task automations creating 'shadow IT' risks.
Migration trigger: VBScript removal from future Windows releases.

content.title: 2026 Decision Framework
content.subtitle: Matching the process to the right target architecture
keyPoints: Low Complexity: Replace with PowerShell or JavaScript/TypeScript.
High Integration: Prioritize SAP APIs (OData/REST) and BTP services.
Legacy Gaps: Use governed Enterprise RPA as a bridge for GUI screens.
Modern Choice: SAP Build Process Automation for business-led workflows.

content.title: Market Momentum: RPA Still Growing
content.subtitle: Bridging the gap for legacy SAP GUI screens
content.message: The RPA market continues to expand at a 29% CAGR, shifting from simple screen scraping to governed automation platforms.

content.title: Low-Code: The New Enterprise Default
content.subtitle: Scaling beyond traditional script maintenance
content.message: Low-code platforms are projected to reach $376B by 2034, offering a governance upgrade for fragile scripts.

content.title: SAP Cloud Momentum Pulls Automation Forward
content.subtitle: Aligning modernization with SAP Q1 2026 results
keyPoints: Cloud ERP Suite revenue growth at +30% reinforces BTP-aligned strategy.
Current cloud backlog reached €21.9B as of early 2026.
Modernization should follow Cloud ERP patterns, not desktop-bound VBScript.
Strategic goal: Gains in market share through AI-driven cloud solutions.

content.title: Target Architecture After VBScript
content.subtitle: A resilient, layered automation stack
keyPoints: Orchestration: SAP Build Process Automation for approvals and rules.
Integration: SAP Integration Suite (REST/OData) as the core engine.
Execution: Tactical use of PowerShell/JS for OS and file tasks.
Intelligence: Joule Studio and AI Core for contextual multi-step tasks.

content.title: Alternative Selection Guide
content.subtitle: Matching workload to replacement pattern
body: PowerShell: Best for file handling and local Windows administration.
JavaScript/TS: Strong fit for BTP extensions and modern web stacks.
SAP Build: Strategic native option for process automation and rules.
Enterprise RPA: Necessary bridge for non-API legacy GUI screens.
Joule Agents: Emerging choice for knowledge-heavy, contextual tasks.

content.title: Migration Roadmap 2026-2027
content.subtitle: Controlled transition before the default-disable milestone
content.message: A phased approach from Discovery to Governance ensures business continuity without disruption.

content.title: Management View: Strategic Gains
content.subtitle: Growing faster than the market with AI

quote.text: We are growing faster than the market and are gaining share as customers expand across our Suite and with our AI solutions.
quote.speaker: Christian Klein
quote.title: CEO, SAP

content.title: Executive Recommendation
content.subtitle: Modernizing the SAP automation window
body: Immediate: Launch global inventory of legacy .vbs and GUI scripts.
Policy: Enforce a 'No New VBScript' mandate for all developers.
Strategic: Pivot to SAP Build and APIs for critical business processes.
Tactical: Use PowerShell/JS as a direct replacement for OS-level tasks.
KPI: Target 80%+ VBScript retirement by the end of 2026.
